commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: connect settings page to Supabase + settings API route

- New /api/settings route: GET loads profile/workspace settings,
  PUT saves name, LLM config, privacy, notifications to Supabase
- Settings page: loads real user data (name, email, LLM config)
  on mount, saves changes via API, shows loading state on buttons
- Dynamic avatar initials from user name
- Remove hardcoded "Jean Dupont" / "jean@example.com"

security: add HTTP security headers and API rate limiting

- next.config.ts: X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff,
  HSTS, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
- Rate limiting: register (5/15min per IP), chat (30/min per user),
  upload (20/min per user) with in-memory store and auto-cleanup
- Returns 429 Too Many Requests when limits exceeded

  1M+ PPS requires Native XDP on i40e/ixgbe, not achievable on virtio

repository.description: My custom Claude Code skills

commit.message:
Rebalance tiers toward discovery, enforce small-cap Tier 3

Stage 1: 1+2+3+3 = 9 nodes (down from 11).
Stage 2: Tier 0 nodes get 2-3 companies; all other tiers get exactly 2.
Added quality/discovery instruction and Tier 3 small-cap guidance to prompt.
Post-processing: Tier 3 >$5B → Tier 2; Tier 2 >$50B → Tier 1.
Target output: 15-20 companies per thesis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add staged AI interface protocol spec (#10)

Defines the network protocol between TronGrid and AI brain clients.
Stage 0 uses scalar sensory channels (energy signatures, contact,
temperature, damage, light) — no rendering pipeline required. Protocol
grows with AI capability through 4 stages up to full visual streaming.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_valid + maxBits_pos — Huffman table validity composition (#940)

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_valid + maxBits_pos — Huffman table validity composition

Lift table validity from the builder level to the parser level:
- parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_build_elim: extract buildZstdHuffmanTable success
  from either branch (direct or FSE)
- parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_valid: returned table satisfies ValidHuffmanTable
- parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_maxBits_pos: returned table has maxBits ≥ 1

These enable downstream proofs about decompressCompressedBlock to assume
table validity without reasoning about the internal dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: decodeHuffmanStreamWF + output_size + totalBitsRemaining — Huffman stream WF refactor and composition theorems (#929)

* feat: decodeHuffmanStreamWF + output_size + totalBitsRemaining — Huffman stream WF refactor and composition theorems (#922)

Add `decodeHuffmanStreamWF`, a well-founded variant of `decodeHuffmanStream`
using structural recursion on `count` (unfoldable in proofs). Prove two
composition theorems:
- `decodeHuffmanStreamWF_size`: output has exactly `acc.size + count` bytes
- `decodeHuffmanStreamWF_totalBitsRemaining_le`: bit budget is monotonically
  non-increasing through stream decoding

* feat: add decodeSequencesWF + output_size — interleaved FSE sequence decoder WF refactor

Add proof-friendly WF variant of decodeSequences that manages three
interleaved FSE states (litLen, matchLen, offset) with structural
recursion on remaining count. Extract decodeOneSequence helper for
symbol lookup and extra bits reading.

Prove decodeSequencesWF_loop_size (output = acc.size + remaining)
and decodeSequencesWF_size (output = numSeq) by induction on remaining.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for decodeSequencesWF session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: skill update — explicit match vs do in WF proof targets

Add guidance on preferring explicit match over do notation in WF
functions intended for proof reasoning. do notation desugars to
Bind.bind which is hard to unfold after dite splits; explicit match
makes split at h predictable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: decodeFourHuffmanStreamsWF + output_size — 4-stream Huffman decoder WF refactor

Add proof-friendly WF variant of decodeFourHuffmanStreams using
decodeHuffmanStreamWF (structural recursion) instead of opaque
decodeHuffmanStream (for loop). Prove that when the WF variant
succeeds, the output has exactly regenSize bytes.

The size theorem requires 3 * ((regenSize + 3) / 4) ≤ regenSize
because Nat subtraction underflows for regenSize ∈ {1, 2, 5}.
In practice Zstd 4-stream mode requires regenSize ≥ 256.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for decodeFourHuffmanStreamsWF session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: parseLiteralsSection raw/RLE content — extract and all-equal characterizations (#952)

* feat: parseLiteralsSection raw/RLE content characterizations

Add two content characterization theorems for parseLiteralsSection:

- parseLiteralsSection_raw_eq_extract: for raw literals (type 0), the
  output is an exact contiguous slice of the input data between the
  end of the variable-width header and pos'.

- parseLiteralsSection_rle_all_eq: for RLE literals (type 1), all
  output bytes are identical, capturing the mathematical essence of
  run-length encoding.

These are characterizing properties (not tautological restations),
providing the base case for block content correctness in the
decompression pipeline.

Closes #946

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for parseLiteralsSection content characterizations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: decompressBlocksWF single-block content — raw and RLE block output characterization (#955)

* feat: decompressBlocksWF single-block content — raw and RLE output characterization

Add two content characterization theorems for `decompressBlocksWF`:
- `decompressBlocksWF_single_raw`: when encountering a single raw last block,
  result is `output ++ block` at the raw block's end position
- `decompressBlocksWF_single_rle`: same for RLE blocks

These are the first content-level theorems for the block loop, bridging
block-level content specs (decompressRawBlock_content, decompressRLEBlock_content)
to the block-loop level. Together with existing structural theorems (size, prefix,
pos_gt, le_size), they establish what the output ByteArray actually contains.

Closes #948

commit.message:
Fix Gemini JSON parser corrupting multi-word store names

The unquoted-keys regex was matching the last word of quoted keys
like "Mosaicos La Peninsular" and replacing with just "Peninsular",
truncating store names. Now tries parsing raw JSON first (since
responseMimeType=json is set), only applies safe repairs on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Canonical Task Model for study-level milestone tracking

Add database models, seed data, and smart task matcher to enable timeline sheet integration and cross-study phase intelligence.

Database Models (Prisma):
- TrialPhase enum: 8 phases (REGULATORY → PLANNING → STARTUP → ENROLLMENT → CONDUCT → COMPLETION → DATA → REPORTING)
- CanonicalTask: 30 standard clinical trial milestones (FDA_SUBMISSION, IRB_APPROVAL, SITE_ACTIVATION, LPLV, DATABASE_LOCK, etc.)
- OrgTaskMapping: Maps user-defined task names from Smartsheet timeline sheets to canonical tasks
- StudyMilestone: Stores extracted milestone data (task name, start/end dates, duration, %complete, assignedTo, predecessor)

Seed Data:
- 30 canonical tasks organized by phase with sequence order
- Covers full trial lifecycle from regulatory submission through publication
- Sweet spot size (25-35 tasks) for manageable mapping without noise

Task Matcher Service:
- Smart matching of user task names to canonical tasks using 3-tier confidence system:
  * Tier 1: Exact match (100% confidence)
  * Tier 2: Substring match (95-99% confidence)
  * Tier 3: Fuzzy match using Levenshtein distance (85-94% confidence)
- Pattern dictionary with common variations for each canonical task
  * Example: "Submit to Central IRB" → IRB_SUBMISSION (98% confidence)
  * Example: "Ethics Committee Approval" → IRB_APPROVAL (96% confidence)
- Similar architecture to existing column matcher for operational signals

API Endpoints (/tasks):
- GET /tasks/match-task?taskName=... - Suggest canonical task for single task name
- POST /tasks/suggest-mappings - Bulk task mapping suggestions for timeline wizard
- GET /tasks/canonical - List all canonical tasks ordered by sequence
- GET /tasks/canonical/by-phase - List tasks grouped by trial phase

Architecture:
- Parallel to site-level signal mapping (Column mapping → Signal extraction → Site snapshots)
- Study-level task mapping (Task mapping → Milestone extraction → Phase metrics)
- Enables cross-study intelligence: "Your startup phase is 47 days vs portfolio average of 32 days"

This solves the Timeline sheet question: Timeline sheets track study milestones (not site operations), and this model provides the infrastructure to map timeline task names to canonical milestones for benchmarking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: catastrophic regex backtracking freezing story generation at 14%

The clothing parser regex had nested quantifiers ((?:[^:\r\n,(]+|\([^)]*\))+)
that caused O(2^n) backtracking when AI generated costumed:{description with
commas} format. This blocked the Node.js event loop indefinitely.

Fix: Use lazy [^:\r\n]+? for name group and handle costumed:{...} braces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: emit SSE event after set_read_mode to sync UI (#140)

After successfully setting read mode via set_config3, emit a
ReaderInfoUpdated SSE event so all connected UI clients see the new mode
immediately.

Previously the UI would default to "raw" until the next 10-second poll
interval. This change mirrors the pattern already used in
set_recording_handler.

Fixes the issue where forwarder UI incorrectly shows "raw" on initial
connect instead of the actual read mode.

Phase 3: Block tracks matching avoid list on venue zones after schedule creation

New function sybBlockTracksForAvoidList():
- Parses avoidList into keywords (same logic as deterministicMatch)
- Searches SYB for tracks matching each keyword (public search, max 3 keywords)
- Blocks each track on all venue zones (max 30 tracks, 100ms delay)
- Uses verified Reason enum value 'other'
- Stores blocked track IDs in schedule_data JSONB for potential cleanup

Integrated into POST /submit after schedule creation:
- Looks up zones via sybGetZones(accountId)
- Non-critical: failures logged but don't break submission
- Only runs when avoidList is present and account is confirmed

commit.message:
perf: parallelize table provisioning with Promise.allSettled

Tables are independent (jsonb mode), no need for sequential creation.
Should significantly reduce build time for complex apps (20+ tables).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.2.4: Fix CUDA DLL download crashing PowerShell on Razer Blade

- Switch cudart download from Invoke-WebRequest to WebClient.DownloadFile.
  Invoke-WebRequest buffers the entire ~500MB response in RAM before writing
  to disk, which can crash the PowerShell process entirely (not a catchable
  exception) on systems with limited free memory.
- Replace ZipFile::ExtractToDirectory with manual per-entry extraction that
  overwrites existing files, avoiding IOException when files already exist
  in BIN_DIR from the main CUDA zip extraction.
- Broaden cublas detection to check System32, CUDA toolkit install dirs,
  and PATH for ANY cublas64 DLL (not just a version-specific match), so
  users with a different CUDA version don't trigger a redundant download.
- Clean up partial zip on download/extract failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add chainaware-lead-scorer subagent

Sales lead qualification engine for Web3 platforms. Scores a wallet 0–100
as a conversion prospect by combining experience (35pts), intent signals
(25pts), activity breadth (20pts), risk appetite (10pts), and a fraud
penalty (−10pts max).

Returns lead tier (Hot/Warm/Cold/Dead), conversion probability, and a
specific outreach angle — including channel fit, timing signal, and what
not to do. Supports product context to boost matching intent signals and
batch mode for ranking full wallet lists by sales potential.

Bumps subagent count to 22 across all docs.

for graceful shutdown.

Fix character save: upload images to Firebase Storage instead of base64

stores only download URLs. Also loads characters from Firebase on init.

commit.message:
Add Phase 4: navigation JS and bilingual i18n system

Build main.js (347 lines) with mobile hamburger menu (overlay, focus
trap, body scroll lock, Escape key), sticky header (.scrolled class
at scrollY>50), smooth scroll with header offset, active nav link
highlighting via IntersectionObserver, and scroll-triggered fade-in
animations on 27 elements (.animate-on-scroll → .visible).

Build i18n.js (562 lines) with complete English/Tamil translations
for all 127 unique keys. Handles data-i18n (textContent),
data-i18n-placeholder (form inputs), and data-i18n-tooltip (floating
buttons). Persists language choice in localStorage, updates html lang
attribute, and toggles .lang-ta body class for Noto Sans Tamil font.

Add supporting CSS: nav overlay, body scroll lock, active nav link
style, .lang-ta font override, .animate-on-scroll with staggered
transition delays. Add .animate-on-scroll to service/doctor/why-us/
blog cards and about section in HTML.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
